title: Value vs Growth Stocks: Performance Comparison (2026)
description: Value vs growth stock performance comparison for 2026. See historical returns, valuations, and an ETF comparison to decide which strategy wins.

mainEntity.name: Are value stocks better than growth stocks?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, value stocks outperformed growth over the very long term (Fama-French model), but growth stocks have dominated the last decade, driven by big tech. The 'better' choice depends entirely on your time horizon and market cycle.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between a value ETF and a growth ETF?

acceptedAnswer.text: A value ETF (like VTV) holds mature companies with low P/E ratios and higher dividend yields. A growth ETF (like VUG) holds companies with rapidly increasing revenue and earnings, usually trading at high P/E ratios with little to no dividends.

mainEntity.name: Why do growth stocks have higher P/E ratios?

acceptedAnswer.text: Investors are willing to pay a premium for growth stocks today because they expect their earnings to grow significantly in the future. You are paying for future potential rather than current profits.

mainEntity.name: Is it better to invest in VTV or VUG?

acceptedAnswer.text: Many investors hold both to create a balanced, blended portfolio (which essentially mimics the S&P 500 or total market). If you want dividend income and lower volatility, VTV is preferable. If you have a long time horizon and can stomach volatility for higher potential returns, VUG is favored.

mainEntity.name: When do value stocks perform best?

acceptedAnswer.text: Value stocks typically perform best during periods of economic recovery, high inflation, or rising interest rates. When rates rise, the 'future cash flows' of growth stocks are discounted more heavily, making the current cash flows of value stocks more attractive.
